summary: Strict kind enforcement (Stage 4) silently no-ops for the two realistic `as image strict` forms — a path literal ("real.gif") and a read-lifted image.@this — because TryInstantiateValidator only builds a probe from raw byte[], and image.ValidateKind only reads byte[]. The C# Cut2 tests pass only because they feed raw bytes; the three PLang .test.goal files that claim to cover the mismatch cases pass while asserting nothing (a PNG is accepted as a strict GIF, build and runtime). Ingi ruled (2026-05-31): validation fires at byte-materialization, wherever that is — if strict, throw. So `set` stays lazy (path-literal sub-claim withdrawn), but nothing currently checks at byte-load: the declared (kind,strict) must ride with the image value to the load seam (BytesAsync / already-loaded set), ValidateKind must accept an image.@this not just byte[], and the mismatch goals need real assertions. F2 (medium): kind is serialized twice — inside the structured `type` entity and as the flat Data.Kind sibling key — OBP smell #6, branch-introduced. F3-F5 minor: type.Scheme null-deref, text-kind gate at call site, dead CanonicaliseKind fast-path. Build clean, C# 3818/3818, PLang 263/263 — green but the strict-image tests are false-confidence.
